subject: Guest Lecture Invitation — Library Science Program Fall 2026
body_preview: Dear Jenny, we would be delighted to have you return as a guest lecturer for our Library Science Program this fall. Would you be available in late October?

subject: Re: Guest Lecture Invitation — Library Science Program Fall 2026
body_preview: Dear Catherine, thank you so much for the kind invitation. I would love to return. October 30th works well for me — shall we say 6 PM?

subject: Re: Guest Lecture Invitation — confirmed Oct 30
body_preview: Wonderful! October 30 at 6 PM is confirmed. I will send along the room assignment and A/V request form shortly. Students are so excited to hear from a practitioner with your depth of experience.

subject: A/V Setup Request Form — Oct 30 Lecture
body_preview: Please complete the attached A/V request form at least 10 days before your scheduled lecture on October 30. Indicate whether you require a projector, lapel microphone, or hybrid Zoom setup.

subject: Lecture Materials Submission — Due October 15
body_preview: Jenny, a gentle reminder that lecture slides, handouts, or reading list should be submitted by October 15 so we can post them to the course portal ahead of time.

subject: Student Roster — LIBSC 602 Fall 2026
body_preview: Attached is the confirmed student roster for LIBSC 602 Community Engagement and Reference Services. There are 18 students enrolled. Please let me know if you have questions about the cohort.

subject: Welcome, Jenny — HGI Guest Lecturer Portal Access
body_preview: Your guest lecturer account has been activated. You may upload materials at https://portal.hartfordgraduateinstitute.edu/guest. Your temporary PIN is enclosed.

subject: Dept Chair Note — Looking Forward to Your Visit
body_preview: Dear Jenny, just a note from the department chair to say how much we value your continued involvement with the program. Your lecture last year on rural library outreach was mentioned in three end-of-term reflections.

subject: Re: Parking and Campus Access on Oct 30
body_preview: Catherine, could you confirm which lot is closest to Whitmore Hall? My spouse Harriet will be dropping me off. Also, is there visitor parking validation?

subject: Re: Parking — Lot C Visitor Passes Enclosed
body_preview: Use Lot C off Farmington Ave — it is a short walk to Whitmore Hall. I have arranged two visitor passes. I will leave them at the front desk under your name.

subject: Fellow Guest Lecturer Introduction — Dr. Yolanda Ferris
body_preview: Jenny, I wanted to introduce you to Dr. Yolanda Ferris who lectures on digital archives. She will be presenting the week before you and thought you two might enjoy connecting beforehand.

subject: Hello from Dr. Yolanda Ferris — HGI Guest Lecturer
body_preview: Hi Jenny, Catherine suggested we get in touch. I lecture on digital preservation and would love to hear about your community outreach work at Welles-Turner. Perhaps we could grab coffee on campus?

subject: Lecture Honorarium — W-9 Form Required
body_preview: To process your honorarium of $250 for the October 30 lecture, please complete and return the enclosed W-9 form at your earliest convenience.

subject: Re: W-9 Submitted — Thank You
body_preview: Hello, I have completed and attached the W-9. Please let me know if anything else is needed. Thank you for the prompt communication.

subject: Lecture Topic Confirmation — Community Reference Services
body_preview: Catherine, as discussed, my lecture title will be 'Meeting Patrons Where They Are: 32 Years of Community Reference at a Small Public Library.' I will circulate a one-page abstract by December 1.

subject: Abstract Received — Perfect Fit for the Curriculum
body_preview: Jenny, the abstract is exactly what we hoped for. It aligns beautifully with our unit on patron-centered services. Students will be required to submit a short response journal entry after your talk.

subject: Prior Year Lecture Recap — For Your Reference
body_preview: I am attaching the student feedback summary from your October 2024 lecture. Average rating was 4.8/5.0. Several students cited your storytelling approach as a highlight.

subject: A/V Request Confirmation — Oct 30 Whitmore 210
body_preview: We have confirmed your A/V setup for October 30, Whitmore Hall Room 210: projector, lapel microphone, and Zoom hybrid link. A technician will be on site by 5:30 PM.

subject: Post-Lecture Thank You — October 2024
body_preview: Dear Catherine, thank you so much for the warm welcome last Tuesday. The students asked wonderful questions and I left feeling genuinely energized. It is always a pleasure to give back to the program that shaped my own career.

subject: Library Science Advisory Committee — Save the Date
body_preview: Jenny, we are forming a new advisory committee of practitioners and would love to include you. The inaugural meeting is October 14 at 2 PM on campus. Purely advisory — no heavy commitment required.

subject: Suggested Reading List for LIBSC 602
body_preview: Attached is the course reading list for LIBSC 602 so you can see what frameworks students will already be familiar with before your lecture. Happy to discuss if you'd like to build on any of it.

subject: Re: Suggested Reading List — Very Helpful
body_preview: Catherine, this is enormously helpful. I see they've covered Durrance and Fisher — I can build on that foundation rather than recapping it. I'll weave in some concrete Glastonbury case studies instead.
